glossary.def: A sequence model built on a linear recurrence from control theory: a fixed-size hidden state is updated from each input token and read out as the output. Because the state never grows, an SSM processes a sequence in time linear in its length and decodes with constant memory, unlike attention's quadratic cost and growing KV cache.

A state-space model whose write, read, and step-size parameters are functions of the current input rather than fixed constants. This input-dependence lets the model choose what to remember and how fast to forget per token, recovering much of attention's content-aware selectivity while keeping linear cost; it is computed on GPUs with a hardware-aware parallel scan.

The Mamba-2 result that a selective SSM (with scalar state decay) and a particular form of structured masked attention compute the same function by two routes. The quadratic 'attention' form is efficient for parallel training; the linear 'recurrent' form is efficient for constant-memory inference. SSD is why 'Transformers are SSMs.'

An attention variant that replaces the softmax with a feature map so the score computation factorizes. Reassociating the matmuls from (QKᵀ)V to Q(KᵀV) turns the cost from O(n²) to O(n) by maintaining a fixed-size running state instead of an n×n score matrix. The trade-off is weaker sharp, content-addressable recall, which gated and delta-rule variants partly restore.

A model that interleaves mostly linear-cost mixers (SSM or linear attention) with a thin fraction of full softmax-attention layers. The linear layers carry the bulk of token mixing cheaply while the few attention layers supply the exact long-range recall that linear mixers lack — the 2026 sweet spot, exemplified by Jamba and Griffin-style designs.

Compressing a model's weights below the practical 4-bit floor — to 3, ~2.x, or even ~1.58 effective bits — where naive uniform rounding fails because of a few large outlier weights. It works only with rotation/incoherence preprocessing and learned codebooks (QuIP#, QuaRot) or by training at low precision (BitNet b1.58), trading model size against quality.

A quantization preprocessing step that multiplies weights and activations by random orthogonal or Hadamard rotations. The rotation preserves the matrix product but spreads concentrated outlier values across all dimensions, making the distribution more uniform and far easier to quantize accurately at very low bit-widths. Core to QuIP#, QuaRot, and SpinQuant.

A Transformer trained from scratch with weights constrained to the ternary set {-1, 0, +1}, about log₂3 ≈ 1.58 bits per weight. It reportedly matches full-precision models at billion-parameter scale while replacing most multiplications with additions; how far the result holds at the very largest scales remains contested.

Katharopoulos, A., Vyas, A., Pappas, N. & Fleuret, F. (2020). Transformers are RNNs: Fast Autoregressive Transformers with Linear Attention. ICML 2020.
Lieber, O. et al. (2024). Jamba: A Hybrid Transformer-Mamba Language Model.
Frantar, E., Ashkboos, S., Hoefler, T. & Alistarh, D. (2022). GPTQ: Accurate Post-Training Quantization for Generative Pre-trained Transformers. ICLR 2023.
